Controlled Terminology and Dictionary Providers

Source: vignettes/dictionaries.Rmd
dictionaries.Rmd

Clinical validation depends on reference data: CDISC controlled terminology, SRS, MedDRA, WHODrug, LOINC, SNOMED, and sponsor dictionaries. herald uses one interface for all of them: a dictionary provider.

Bundled CDISC CT

The package ships bundled SDTM and ADaM controlled terminology.

ct_info("sdtm")
#> $package
#> [1] "sdtm"
#> 
#> $version
#> [1] "2026-03-27"
#> 
#> $release_date
#> [1] "2026-03-27"
#> 
#> $row_count
#> [1] 45612
#> 
#> $codelist_count
#> [1] 1215
#> 
#> $source_path
#> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/herald/rules/ct/sdtm-ct.rds"
#> 
#> $source_url
#> [1] NA
ct_info("adam")
#> $package
#> [1] "adam"
#> 
#> $version
#> [1] "2026-03-27"
#> 
#> $release_date
#> [1] "2026-03-27"
#> 
#> $row_count
#> [1] 144
#> 
#> $codelist_count
#> [1] 25
#> 
#> $source_path
#> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/herald/rules/ct/adam-ct.rds"
#> 
#> $source_url
#> [1] NA

Load the raw CT when you need direct inspection:

sdtm_ct <- load_ct("sdtm")

length(sdtm_ct)
#> [1] 1215
names(sdtm_ct)[1:10]
#>  [1] "TENMW1TC"      "TENMW1TN"      "A4STR1TC"      "A4STR1TN"     
#>  [5] "D4STR1TC"      "D4STR1TN"      "SIXMW1TC"      "SIXMW1TN"     
#>  [9] "AIMS0101T07OR" "AIMS0108T09OR"

sdtm_ct[["NY"]]$terms[, c("submissionValue", "preferredTerm")]
#>   submissionValue  preferredTerm
#> 1               N             No
#> 2              NA Not Applicable
#> 3               U        Unknown
#> 4               Y            Yes

CT as a provider

Use ct_provider() when rule operations need dictionary lookup behavior.

p <- ct_provider("sdtm")

p
#> <herald_dict_provider>
#>   name     : ct-sdtm
#>   version  : 2026-03-27
#>   source   : bundled
#>   license  : CC-BY-4.0
#>   size     : 45,612 rows
#>   fields   : TENMW1TC, TENMW1TN, A4STR1TC, A4STR1TN, D4STR1TC, D4STR1TN, SIXMW1TC, SIXMW1TN, AIMS0101T07OR, AIMS0108T09OR, AIMS0110OR, AIMS0111T12OR, AIMS0101T07STR, AIMS0108T09STR, AIMS0110STR, AIMS0111T12STR, AIMS01TC, AIMS01TN, ACCPARTY, ACN, ACSPCAT, APCH101OR, APCH102OR, APCH103OR, APCH104OR, APCH105AOR, APCH105BOR, APCH106AOR, APCH106BOR, APCH107OR, APCH108OR, APCH109OR, APCH110OR, APCH111OR, APCH114OR, APCH115OR, APCH101STR, APCH102STR, APCH103STR, APCH104STR, APCH105ASTR, APCH105BSTR, APCH106ASTR, APCH106BSTR, APCH107STR, APCH108STR, APCH109STR, APCH110STR, APCH111STR, APCH114STR, APCH115STR, APCH1TC, APCH1TN, ADTNQRS, AVL01TC, AVL01TN, AGEU, AQ01TC, AQ01TN, AJCC1TC, AJCC1TN, AJCCGRD, ADTC01TC, ADTC01TN, ADT01TC, ADT01TN, AVPU01TC, AVPU01TN, ADCTC, ADCTN, ADL03TC, ADL03TN, ADL01TC, ADL01TN, ADL02TC, ADL02TN, ACGC01TC, ACGC01TN, LOC, AVOUTTRT, APGR01TC, APGR01TN, ASEX01TC, ASEX01TN, ASEX02TC, ASEX02TN, ARMNULRS, ASSG01TC, ASSG01TN, ADSD01TC, ADSD01TN, ASFATSCD, ASFATS, ANSD01TC, ANSD01TN, ASCVD1TC, ASCVD1TN, ATLAS101OR, ATLAS102OR, ATLAS103OR, ATLAS104OR, ATLAS105OR, ATLAS101STR, ATLAS102STR, ATLAS103STR, ATLAS104STR, ATLAS105STR, ATLAS1TC, ATLAS1TN, AUTESTCD, AUTEST, BEBQ01TC, BEBQ01TN, BEBQ02TC, BEBQ02TN, BARS01TC, BARS01TN, BDI01TC, BDI01TN, ISBDAGT, BSTESTCD, BSTEST, BEDECOD, BLCS01TC, BLCS01TN, BCR101TC, BCR101TN, BWCS01TC, BWCS01TN, BRDGMOOD, BACS1TC, BACS1TN, BPI1TC, BPI1TN, BPI2TC, BPI2TN, BPRS01TC, BPRS01TN, BPRSA118OR, BPRSA1SET1OR, BPRSA1SET2OR, BPRSA118STR, BPRSA1SET1STR, BPRSA1SET2STR, BPRSA1TC, BPRSA1TN, BUERS1TC, BUERS1TN, CCSGA1TC, CCSGA1TN, CCSGA101OR, CCSGA101STR, CVPRCIND, CRYDFMAN, CVFARS, CVFATSCD, CVFATS, CVTESTCD, CVTEST, CASEFIND, CPCAT, CCCAT, DECAT, DSCAT, FTCAT, IECAT, ONCRSCAT, QSCAT, CDFATSCD, CDFATS, CHIVC1TC, CHIVC1TN, CTAUGRS, CPTESTCD, CPTEST, CELSTATE, CPS01TC, CPS01TN, CDRS1TC, CDRS1TN, CRQ01TC, CRQ01TN, CRQ02TC, CRQ02TN, CCQ02TC, CCQ02TN, CCQ01TC, CCQ01TN, CDR01TC, CDR01TN, CGI02TC, CGI02TN, COWS1TC, COWS1TN, CAP01TC, CAP01TN, COLSTYP, CSS05TC, CSS05TN, CSS01TC, CSS01TN, CSS03TC, CSS03TN, CSS04TC, CSS04TN, CSS06TC, CSS06TN, CSS07TC, CSS07TN, CSS08TC, CSS08TN, CSS11TC, CSS11TN, CSS10TC, CSS10TN, CSS09TC, CSS09TN, CSS02TC, CSS02TN, CES0101OR, CES0102OR, CES0103OR, CES0104OR, CES0105T07OR, CES0101STR, CES0102STR, CES0103STR, CES0104STR, CES0105T07STR, CES01TC, CES01TN, CBS01TC, CBS01TN, CMSPSTAT, NCOMPLT, CSTATE, CCRCLSCD, CCRCLS, CCINVTYP, CONROL, TCNTRL, COWAT1TC, COWAT1TN, CPFATSCD, CPFATS, CADPRSN, CADRISK, CADSYMP, CADG01TC, CADG01TN, CADGRS, CARTDOM, TIMG01TC, TIMG01TN, CTTIMIRS, CVSLDEXT, COVI01TC, COVI01TN, C19FATCD, C19FAT, CHSF1TC, CHSF1TN, CHSF2TC, CHSF2TN, CDAI01TC, CDAI01TN, CRFATSCD, CRFATS, CLTMDTYP, COMM1TC, COMM1TN, DRRI1TC, DRRI1TN, DLQI1TC, DLQI1TN, DEACNDEV, DIPARM, DIPARMCD, DOTESTCD, DOTEST, DTDECOD, DURS, DUTESTCD, DUTEST, DDS01TC, DDS01TN, DDS02TC, DDS02TN, DDS03TC, DDS03TN, DIABTHPY, DKFATSCD, DKFATS, DIBC01TC, DIBC01TN, DICTNAM, DIR, DAD01TC, DAD01TN, DRS01TC, DRS01TN, DISCHDX, DSSOUT, DSPRTYP, DS01TC, DS01TN, DATESTCD, DATEST, DRSTAT, DMFATSCD, DMFATS, ECOG101OR, ECOG101STR, ECOG1TC, ECOG1TN, EBFATSCD, EBFATS, EGLEAD, EGRDMETH, EGSTRESC, TWOFFMTH, EGTESTCD, EGMETHOD, EGTEST, EPDS01TC, EPDS01TN, ETRP01TC, ETRP01TN, EMO1TC, EMO1TN, EMPSTAT, SETTING, EPOCH, ESS01TC, ESS01TN, ETHNIC, ETHNICC, EOR06TC, EOR06TN, EOR01TC, EOR01TN, EOR18TC, EOR18TN, EOR02TC, EOR02TN, EQ5D02TC, EQ5D02TN, EQ5D01TC, EQ5D01TN, EVAL, EXACT1TC, EXACT1TN, ED1TC, ED1TN, ED2TC, ED2TN, GOSE1TC, GOSE1TN, ESRSA1TC, ESRSA1TN, FPSR1TC, FPSR1TN, FES01TC, FES01TN, FSS01TC, FSS01TN, FDATSRS, FATESTCD, FATEST, SKINCLAS, FCVD1TC, FCVD1TN, FREQ, FAQ01TC, FAQ01TN, FAC065TC, FAC065TN, FAC062TC, FAC062TN, FAC006TC, FAC006TN, FAC061TC, FAC061TN, FAC074TC, FAC074TN, FAC029TC, FAC029TN, FAC005TC, FAC005TN, FAC075TC, FAC075TN, FAC008TC, FAC008TN, FAC015TC, FAC015TN, FAC039TC, FAC039TN, FAC041TC, FAC041TN, FAC017TC, FAC017TN, FAC043TC, FAC043TN, FAC016TC, FAC016TN, FAC018TC, FAC018TN, FAC049TC, FAC049TN, FAC063TC, FAC063TN, FAC057TC, FAC057TN, FAC084TC, FAC084TN, FAC100TC, FAC100TN, FAC070TC, FAC070TN, FAC071TC, FAC071TN, FAC101TC, FAC101TN, CPS0101OR, CPS0102OR, CPS0103OR, CPS0104OR, CPS0105AOR, CPS0105BOR, CPS0107OR, CPS0101STR, CPS0102STR, CPS0103STR, CPS0104STR, CPS0105ASTR, CPS0105BSTR, CPS0107STR, FTCD01TC, FTCD01TN, FAC102TC, FAC102TN, GENIDENT, IGDCMPLX, ISXDXRS, NY1TC, NY1TN, PA215TC, PA215TN, PA020TC, PA020TN, PA261TC, PA261TN, SEXABRTH, SEXORIRS, PDPARMCD, PDPARM, SPECPT, PTTESTCD, PTTEST, ADC1TC, ADC1TN, CLINSTRS, FAC019TC, FAC019TN, MSSTRESC, MELD02TC, MELD02TN, NOTGRDRS, PHQ0101T09OR, PHQ0110OR, PHQ0101T09STR, PHQ0110STR, TPCATRS, EGANMET, EOR30TC, EOR30TN, EOR03TC, EOR03TN, EOR35TC, EOR35TN, EOR24TC, EOR24TN, EOR29TC, EOR29TN, EOR12TC, EOR12TN, EOR21TC, EOR21TN, EOR36TC, EOR36TN, EOR17TC, EOR17TN, EOR19TC, EOR19TN, EOR28TC, EOR28TN, EOR20TC, EOR20TN, ARCI01OR, ARCI01STR, ARCI01TC, ARCI01TN, ADTC0301OR, ADTC0302OR, ADTC0303OR, ADTC03TC, ADTC03TN, ADTC0301STR, ADTC0302STR, ADTC0303STR, ADTC0201OR, ADTC0202OR, ADTC0203OR, ADTC02TC, ADTC02TN, ADTC0201STR, ADTC0202STR, ADTC0203STR, DVCAT, DSQ1TC, DSQ1TN, EATESTCD, EATEST, FACT104TC, FACT104TN, FAC032TC, FAC032TN, DVDECOD
p$contains(c("Y", "N", "UNKNOWN"), field = "NY")
#> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE
p$contains("y", field = "NY", ignore_case = TRUE)
#> [1] TRUE

p$lookup(c("Y", "N"), field = "NY")[, c("submissionValue", "preferredTerm")]
#>   submissionValue preferredTerm
#> 1               N            No
#> 4               Y           Yes

Registering providers

Register providers globally for the R session, or pass them directly to validate(dictionaries = ...) for one run.

register_dictionary("ct-sdtm", ct_provider("sdtm"))
list_dictionaries()
#> # A tibble: 1 × 5
#>   name    version    source  license   size_rows
#>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>   <chr>         <int>
#> 1 ct-sdtm 2026-03-27 bundled CC-BY-4.0     45612
unregister_dictionary("ct-sdtm")

One-run registration avoids session state:

dm <- data.frame(USUBJID = "S1", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

result <- validate(
  files = dm,
  rules = character(0),
  dictionaries = list("ct-sdtm" = ct_provider("sdtm")),
  quiet = TRUE
)

result
#> ── herald validation -- Spec Checks Only ──────────────────────────────
#> Rules: 0/0 applied
#> Datasets checked: 1
#> Findings: 0
#> Duration: 0.02690029 secs

Custom dictionaries

Use custom_provider() when your dictionary is already in a data frame.

sponsor_terms <- data.frame(
  VISIT = c("SCREENING", "BASELINE", "WEEK 1"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

visit_provider <- custom_provider(
  sponsor_terms,
  name = "sponsor-visits",
  fields = "VISIT"
)

visit_provider
#> <herald_dict_provider>
#>   name     : sponsor-visits
#>   version  : unknown
#>   source   : sponsor
#>   license  : sponsor-private
#>   size     : 3 rows
#>   fields   : VISIT
visit_provider$contains(c("BASELINE", "EARLY TERMINATION"), field = "VISIT")
#> [1]  TRUE FALSE

For full control, build a provider directly with new_dict_provider().

allowed <- c("LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH")

risk_provider <- new_dict_provider(
  name      = "risk-levels",
  version   = "2026-04",
  source    = "sponsor",
  license   = "internal",
  size_rows = length(allowed),
  fields    = "RISK",
  contains  = function(value, field = NULL, ignore_case = FALSE) {
    value <- as.character(value)
    if (isTRUE(ignore_case)) {
      return(toupper(value) %in% toupper(allowed))
    }
    value %in% allowed
  }
)

risk_provider$contains(c("LOW", "UNKNOWN"), field = "RISK")
#> [1]  TRUE FALSE

External dictionaries

The package also exposes factories for common clinical dictionaries:

Provider Function
SRS download_srs(), srs_provider()
MedDRA meddra_provider()
WHODrug whodrug_provider()
LOINC loinc_provider()
SNOMED snomed_provider()

Those functions expect licensed or downloaded source files and are usually configured in project-specific setup scripts rather than inside analysis code.

On this page

Developed by Vignesh.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
